page: Amazon Web Services m5.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are included in this configuration, and network connectivity is limited to a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations place the m5.xlarge predominantly in the lower performance tiers. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Similarly, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively low at 26.12 GB/sec for read operations. However, the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating, indicating stable memory subsystem performance relative to its hardware class. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags such as AES, AVX, and AVX512F.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a standard entry point for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-performance compute or local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is its lower benchmark-per-resource efficiency, as demonstrated by weak scores in database operations, static web serving throughput, and LLM inference.

This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads with moderate resource demands. Appropriate applications include small-scale web serving, basic database operations, development environments, and lightweight background utility tasks that do not require high sequential read/write speeds or accelerated graphics processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates that the CPU and memory bandwidth performance generally fall into the bottom 25% tier, though its Passmark memory score remains average. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the m5.xlarge is designed for moderate general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for development environments, small-scale web serving, and basic database operations where high-performance computing and local storage are not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suited for moderate utility workloads.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio for moderate utility and development workloads.

Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
